name: django-mapengine

metadata.name: django-mapengine
metadata.summary: Map engine for maplibre in django

documentation:
# Django-Mapengine

`django-mapengine` is a Django app to provide library for maplibre in backend.
This includes
- html templates and JS files for maplibre
- creation of maplibre sources and layers including choropleths,
- provision of multi-vector-tiles from django models,
- distilling of map source views
- basic popups

## Requirements

Maplibre must be installed (i.e. via npm) and provided as JS framework

## Quick start

1. Add "django_mapengine" to your INSTALLED_APPS setting like this:
   ```python
   INSTALLED_APPS = [
        "...",
        'django_mapengine',
   ]
   ```
2. Install maplibre-gl and pubsub-js dependency by:
   ```shell
   npm install maplibre-gl pubsub-js
   ```
   and copy JS and CSS to your static folder.

3. Include URLs from django_mapengine to your project:
   ```python
   urlpatterns = [
       "...",
       path("map/", include("django_mapengine.urls")),
   ]
   ```

4. Configure map engine by setting zoom levels, regions and styles folder in project settings.py.
   You can see all possible settings by looking into `django_mapengine.settings.py`.
   Example settings:

   ```python
   from django_mapengine import setup

   MAP_ENGINE_CENTER_AT_STARTUP = [12.537917858911896, 51.80812518969171]
   MAP_ENGINE_ZOOM_AT_STARTUP = 9
   MAP_ENGINE_MAX_BOUNDS = [[11.280733017118229, 51.22918643452503], [13.616574868700604, 52.35515806663738]]

   MAP_ENGINE_IMAGES = [setup.MapImage("wind", "images/icons/i_wind.png")]

   MAP_ENGINE_API_MVTS = {
       "municipality":
           [
               setup.MVTAPI("municipality", "map", "Municipality"),
               setup.MVTAPI("municipalitylabel", "map", "Municipality", "label_tiles"),
           ],
       "results": [setup.MVTAPI("results", "map", "Municipality")]
   }

   MAP_ENGINE_API_CLUSTERS = [
       setup.ClusterAPI("wind", "map", "WindTurbine"),
       setup.ClusterAPI("pvroof", "map", "PVroof"),
       setup.ClusterAPI("pvground", "map", "PVground"),
       setup.ClusterAPI("hydro", "map", "Hydro"),
       setup.ClusterAPI("biomass", "map", "Biomass"),
       setup.ClusterAPI("combustion", "map", "Combustion"),
   ]

   MAP_ENGINE_STYLES_FOLDER = "digiplan/static/config/"
   MAP_ENGINE_ZOOM_LEVELS = {
      "municipality": core.Zoom(8, 12),
   }

   MAP_ENGINE_POPUPS = ["results"]
   ```

5. Add middleware to your middleware setup before Whitenoise middleware (or other static server middleware):
   ```python
   MIDDLEWARE = [
       "django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware",
       "django_mapengine.django_mapengine.middleware.MapEngineMiddleware",
       "whitenoise.middleware.WhiteNoiseMiddleware",
       ...
   ]
   ```

6. Add maplibre-gl, pubsub-js and mapengine JS, CSS and JSONs in template by:
   ```html
   {% block javascript %}
     {{ block.super }}
     {% compress js %}
       <script src="{% static 'vendors/maplibre/js/maplibre-gl.js' %}"></script>
       <script src="{% static 'vendors/pubsub/js/pubsub.js' %}"></script>
     {% endcompress %}
   {% endblock javascript %}

   {%  block inline_javascript %}
     {% include 'django_mapengine/map_json.html' %}
     {% compress js %}
       {% include 'django_mapengine/map_js.html' %}
     {% endcompress %}
   {% endblock %}

   {% block css %}
     {% compress css %}
       <link href="{% static 'vendors/maplibre/css/maplibre-gl.css' %}" rel='stylesheet'/>
     {% endcompress %}
   {% endblock css %}
   ```

7. If you want to integrate basemaps to your map add the following to the corresponding places:
   ```html
   <script src="{% static 'django_mapengine/js/basemaps.js' %}" type="text/javascript"></script>
   {% include 'django_mapengine/map_basemaps.html'}
   ```

# User Guides

- [How to define layers](docs/LAYERS.md)
- [How to enable popups](docs/POPUPS.md)
- [How to set up clusters](docs/CLUSTERS.md)

modules.name: django_mapengine.utils
documentation: Various function to be used in other modules

functions.name: django_mapengine.utils.get_color
documentation:
Return color of given layer

Parameters
----------
layer_id : str
    Layer ID to lookup up in layer styles

Returns
-------
Union[str, dict]
    Color of given layer

Raises
------
LookupError
    if color is not found in layer style

functions.name: django_mapengine.utils.get_layer_style
documentation:
Return layer style of given layer ID

Parameters
----------
layer_id : str
    Layer ID to lookup style

Returns
-------
dict
    Layer style of given ID

Raises
------
LookupError
    if layer ID is not found in styles file

modules.name: django_mapengine.sources
documentation: Defines sources in backend to use with maplibre in frontend.

functions.name: django_mapengine.sources.get_static_sources
documentation:
Return sources for all MVTs other than region- or cluster-based

If distilling is used, a second map source for each API is yield, regarding the distilled MVTs.

Yields
------
MapSource
    for each MVT API which is not a region

functions.name: django_mapengine.sources.get_cluster_sources
documentation:
Return geojson sources for all clusters

Yields
------
MapSource
    for each cluster

functions.name: django_mapengine.sources.get_satellite_sources
documentation:
Return source for satellite basemap

Returns
-------
MapSource
    of satellite raster

functions.name: django_mapengine.sources.get_all_sources
documentation:
Return all map sources for regions, satellite, statics and clusters

Returns
-------
List[MapSource]
    all map sources

methods.name: django_mapengine.sources.ClusterMapSource.get_source
documentation:
Return source data for clustering.

Parameters
----------
request: HttpRequest
    Django request holding host and port

Returns

dict

Map source for clustered layers.

modules.name: django_mapengine.choropleth
documentation: Module to handle choropleths.

classes.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.ChoroplethError
documentation: Raised if something is wrong with choropleth values or parameters.

classes.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.__init__
documentation:
Initialize choropleth.

Parameters
----------
choropleth_styles_file: str
    Name or path to choropleth style file

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.get_config
documentation:
Reads config for given name

Return s default config if name is not found.

Parameters
----------
name: str
    Name of choropleth to look up in choropleth config file

Returns
-------
dict
    containing choropleth config

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.get_static_styles
documentation:
Return choropleth styles for static (fixed values) choropleths.

Returns
-------
Dict[str, list]
    Dictionary of fill colors for each static choropleth

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.__calculate_steps
documentation:
Calculate needed steps, either from given values or from static values in choropleth config.

Parameters
----------
choropleth_config : dict
    holding choropleth config
values : Optional[list]
    List with dynamic values (i.e. from simulation)

Returns
-------
list[float]
    Steps to use in choropleth color style

Raises
------
ChoroplethError
    If values are out of range or invalid.
    If values are neither given nor set in config.

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.get_fill_color
documentation:
Return fill_color in choropleth style for setPaintProperty of maplibre.

Parameters
----------
name: str
    Name of choropleth
values: Optional
    Values must be given either dynamically as parameter or must be present as static values in choropleths

Returns
-------
dict:
    Dictionary which can be used as fill_color in maplibre layer

Raises
------
ChoroplethError
    if `color_palette` key is invalid
IndexError
    if values exceed colorbrewer steps

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.__calculate_step_size
documentation:
Calculate step size

Algorithm tries to find nice step sizes instead of simply dividing range by number of steps.

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.__calculate_lower_limit
documentation:
Calculate a significant number as lower limit for choropleth coloring

Parameters
----------
number: float
    find lower limit for this number

Returns
-------
int
    rounded down value by meaningful amount

Raises
------
ValueError
    if lower limit cannot be found

methods.name: django_mapengine.choropleth.Choropleth.__calculate_upper_limit
documentation:
Calculate a significant number as upper limit for choropleth coloring.

Parameters
----------
number: float
    find upper limit for this number

Returns
-------
int
    rounded up value by meaningful amount, depending on the size of number

Raises
------
ValueError
    if upper limit cannot be found
Class to define load choropleth config and define colors for static and dynamic choropleths.

modules.name: django_mapengine.layers
documentation: Defines layers in backend to use with maplibre in frontend.

functions.name: django_mapengine.layers.get_basemap_layers
documentation: Return basemap layers

functions.name: django_mapengine.layers.get_static_layers
documentation:
Return model layers for static-based MVTs.

As multiple layers can have same source (via source_layer), API_MVTs is a dict where key is used as parent source.
Additionally, sources and source layers are reused if same model manager is used for multiple layers.

Yields
------
StaticModelLayer
    Static model layers to show models on map.

functions.name: django_mapengine.layers.get_cluster_layers
documentation:
Return clustered model layers for preparing geojsons.

Yields
------
ClusterModelLayer
    Clustered model layers to show on map.

functions.name: django_mapengine.layers.get_layer_by_id
documentation:
Search for layer API defined in settings

Parameters
----------
layer_id : str
    ID/Name of the layer

Returns
-------
ModelAPI
    API of a model source

Raises
------
KeyError
    if layer ID cannot be found

functions.name: django_mapengine.layers.get_all_layers
documentation:
Return region, static and cluster layers as list

Returns
-------
List[MapLayer]
    List of all region, static and cluster layers

functions.name: django_mapengine.layers.get_layer_style
documentation:
Return layer style for given layer name

Parameters

layer_name: str
    Layer name to look up style for

Returns

dict

Raises
------
KeyError

Defines a layer by using a django model.

classes.name: django_mapengine.layers.StaticModelLayer

methods.name: django_mapengine.layers.StaticModelLayer.get_min_zoom
documentation:
Return minimal zoom. Depends on whether distilling is activated or not.

Parameters
----------
distill : bool
    Whether or not distilling is activated.

Returns
-------
int
    Minimal zoom

methods.name: django_mapengine.layers.StaticModelLayer.get_max_zoom
documentation:
Return maximal zoom. Depends on whether distilling is activated or not.

If distilling is activated, distilled source is used until MAX_DISTILLED_ZOOM,
otherwise zooming goes up to MAX_ZOOM.

Parameters
----------
distill : bool
    Whether or not distilling is activated.

Returns
-------
int
    Maximal zoom

methods.name: django_mapengine.layers.StaticModelLayer.get_style
documentation: Return style from style name if set, otherwise use ID to get style.

methods.name: django_mapengine.layers.StaticModelLayer.get_map_layers
documentation:
Return map layers based on model and distill setting.

Yields
-------
MapLayer
    Static map layer is always returned. Distilled map layer is returned if distilling is active.
Defines a static layer based on a model.

classes.name: django_mapengine.layers.ClusterModelLayer

methods.name: django_mapengine.layers.ClusterModelLayer.get_map_layers
documentation:
Return map layers for clustered model data.

One for unclustered points (original data), one for drawing clustered points and
one for writing number of clusterd points.

Yields
-------
MapLayer
    To be shown in maplibre
Holds logic for clustered layers from django models.

documentation: Module holding helper functions for MVT destillation.

functions.name: django_mapengine.distill.get_coordinates_for_distilling
documentation:
Return x,y,z coordinates for each layer in order to distill it.

Yields
------
tuple[int, int, int]
    Holding x,y,z

functions.name: django_mapengine.distill.get_all_statics_for_state_lod
documentation:
Return distill coordinates for given layer.

Yields
------
tuple[int, int, int]
    Holding x,y,z

classes.name: django_mapengine.legend.LegendLayer

methods.name: django_mapengine.legend.LegendLayer.get_color
documentation:
Return color to show on legend. If color is not set, color is tried to be read from layer style.

Returns
-------
str
    Color string (name/rgb/hex/etc.) to be used on legend in frontend.

methods.name: django_mapengine.legend.LegendLayer.style
documentation:
Return layer style

Returns
-------
dict
    layer style
Define a legend item with color which can activate a layer from model in map.

methods.name: django_mapengine.middleware.MapEngineMiddleware.__call__
documentation:
Checks if request is for a distilled MVT and if response is okay.
Otherwise, it returns empty 204 response.
Catches non-existing MVTs and returns empty HTTPResponses instead

modules.name: django_mapengine.colorbrewer
documentation:
Module to define default choropleth colors from colorbrewer schema

Before, the colorbrewer pacakge was used, but as this package depends on
package "bunch" which is outdated and pacakge seems to be abandoned,
I copied colors from colorbrewer into this module.
